header.description: Two-ship V-n limit / stall scenario that pins the B3 load-factor limiter (seal v1.7r0). AC0 (Ki-61) enters a hard banked turn at g=2.0 with low throttle: as induced drag bleeds speed the C_Lmax aerodynamic ceiling n_aero=cl_max*qS/(m*g0) falls through the commanded 2.0 (the corner speed), so the achievable load factor collapses (2.0 -> ~0.56) and the turn rate dies = accelerated stall; it mushes near V_MIN then unloads to wings-level full throttle and recovers. AC1 (P-47D) does wings-level g-limit zooms: a g=10 command is capped at the structural n_max_struct (8.5), trading speed for altitude, with push-overs between pulls. Together they exercise BOTH clamp branches (n_aero above vs below n_max_struct) and BOTH binds (aerodynamic on AC0, structural on AC1). Both stay well inside +/-90 deg flight-path angle (max ~50 deg / ~67 deg) so the cos(gamma) singularity is never approached. NEW golden for B3.
